summary: Ukrainian forces reported a surge of Shahed and kamikaze drone attacks across multiple fronts, while Russian air defenses claimed to down hundreds of Ukrainian drones. Russia intensified strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure and launched attacks on the Moscow region, including a reported Iskander‑M missile threat from Crimea. Casualties continue to rise on both sides, with civilian deaths reaching over 50 this week.
key_points: Shahed attacks intensified in Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv and Kyiv regions.
Ukrainian President reports >3,170 drones, >1,300 kamikazes and 74 missiles used by Russia this week.
Russian strikes hit Zaporizhzhia NPP transport facilities and a radar station "Raduga".
Ukrainian drones struck multiple targets in Moscow region, including Angstrom plant and oil depots.
Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from occupied Crimea warned by Ukrainian radar.
Civilian casualties this week: 52 killed, 346 injured, including 22 children.
Ukrainian air defense claimed 279/287 Russian drones shot down overnight.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian Shahed and kamikaze drone activity surged, hitting Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv and Kyiv oblasts, while Russian air defenses reported downing nearly 280 drones overnight. Russia expanded its strike envelope, targeting Ukrainian infrastructure and launching attacks deep into the Moscow region, with a warned Iskander‑M launch from Crimea. Civilian toll climbs, with 52 deaths and 346 injuries reported this week.

points: Shahed attacks were recorded from Herson and Zaporizhzhia into Dnipropetrovsk district, with 9 + 3 Shaheds reported, plus additional strikes on Kamianets, Chernihiv (Priluksky) and Kyiv (Vyshhorod) districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have downed 279 of 287 Russian drones during the night, indicating high air‑defence activity (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
A group of UAVs from the Black Sea was reported heading south into Odesa region (Vylkove) suggesting continued maritime drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/62110).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported a threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles being launched from occupied Crimea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66773).

Ukrainian President Zelensky noted over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300+ kamikazes and 74 missiles used by Russia this week, with 52 civilian deaths and 346 injuries, including 22 children (Source: @UkraineNow/81272).
Russian forces bombarded the transport workshop of Zaporizhzhia NPP and the "Raduga" substation with a UAV, according to TASS (Source: @tass_agency/376273).

Ukrainian drones struck the Angstrom micro‑electronics plant, oil depot Sólnečnogorskaya, and the "Raduga" missile plant in Dubna within Moscow region; three Russians killed, at least 16 wounded (Source: @UkraineNow/81265).
Russian air‑defence in Moscow region remains the densest, with over 500 km distance from Ukrainian border, yet Ukrainian strikes continue to penetrate (Source: @UkraineNow/81273).
A reported fire at the Sheremetyevo airport area followed Ukrainian drone impact, indicating deep‑strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81265).
Russian authorities warned of possible Iskander‑M launches from Crimea, highlighting escalation of ballistic missile use (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66773).
Multiple Shahed drones were launched from Sumy region into Chernihiv (Priluksky) and from Kherson into Dnipropetrovsk district, showing cross‑front UAV mobility (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Kamikaze drones were reported over Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk oblasts, indicating sustained pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62118).
Ukrainian air‑defence intercepted 8 strike UAVs across seven locations in the east, with debris falling in multiple settlements (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
A group of UAVs from Kursk region was observed heading toward Sumy and then toward Chernihiv, suggesting Russian offensive UAV deployment from the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/62128).

Russian strikes on Brovary district (Kyiv region) and Kropyvnytskyi caused infrastructure damage but no casualties, reflecting spill‑over effects on eastern‑adjacent zones (Source: @UkraineNow/81276).
Two controlled Shahed drones were seen circling near Boryspil airport, indicating a direct threat to Kyiv's main air hub (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66778).
Ukrainian radar reported a Shahed from Sumy heading to Chernihiv (Priluksky) and another from Chernihiv toward Kyiv (Vyshhorod) (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Groups of UAVs from Chernihiv were observed moving toward Kyiv region, highlighting pressure on the northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62117).
A single Shahed was reported over Dnipro from the south, suggesting coordinated north‑south strike vectors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66790).
Ukrainian air‑defence claimed to have downed multiple UAVs over the northern sector, contributing to the 279/287 total interceptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Ukrainian drones attacked oil facilities in the Moscow region, but also Ukrainian forces reported UAV incursions over the Black Sea heading to Odesa and Mykolaiv regions (Source: @eRadarrua/92202).
A group of UAVs from the Black Sea was directed southward toward Odesa (Vylkove), indicating continued pressure on southern logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62110).
Russian strikes hit the Brovary district (Kyiv region) and Kropyvnytskyi, causing fires and infrastructure damage, showing southern‑west Russian artillery reach (Source: @UkraineNow/81276).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike on Dnipro from the south, highlighting cross‑regional UAV threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66790).
Casualties in the south remain lower, but Ukrainian officials noted eight injured in Dnipropetrovsk from Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported shooting down 279 of 287 Russian drones overnight, reflecting high‑intensity UAV combat (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russian UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia NPP transport facilities and the "Raduga" substation demonstrate use of drones for critical infrastructure targeting (Source: @tass_agency/376273).
Ukrainian drones penetrated deep into Moscow region, striking the Angstrom plant, oil depots and the Sheremetyevo airport area (Source: @UkraineNow/81265).

Multiple Shahed drones were observed over Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv and Kyiv oblasts, with some circling Boryspil airport, indicating persistent low‑altitude threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66778, @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Finland identified four UAVs on its territory as Ukrainian, highlighting the broader regional air‑space implications (Source: @tass_agency/376260).

key_developments: Shahed drones launched from Herson and Zaporizhzhia struck Dnipropetrovsk district, with nine and three Shaheds respectively, plus additional attacks on Kamianets, Chernihiv and Kyiv districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).

Ukrainian President reported Russia used over 3,170 strike drones, 1,300+ kamikazes and 74 missiles this week, causing 52 civilian deaths and 346 injuries, including 22 children (Source: @UkraineNow/81272).
Russian UAVs hit Zaporizhzhia NPP transport workshop and the "Raduga" substation, demonstrating targeting of energy infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/376273).

Ukrainian drones struck the Angstrom micro‑electronics plant, oil depot Sólnečnogorskaya and the Sheremetyevo airport area in Moscow region, killing three Russians and wounding at least 16 (Source: @UkraineNow/81265).
Ukrainian air‑defence claimed 279 of 287 Russian drones were shot down overnight, indicating high‑intensity air‑defence activity (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
A warning of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from occupied Crimea was issued, raising the risk of long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66773).
Two controlled Shahed drones were observed circling Boryspil airport, posing a direct threat to Kyiv's main air hub (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66778).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian Shahed and kamikaze drone operations have expanded, targeting critical nodes across Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv and Kyiv oblasts, while Russian UAV strikes focus on energy and logistics assets. The reported Iskander‑M launch capability from Crimea introduces a new ballistic threat, potentially extending the reach of Russian strikes. Both sides' air‑defence systems are under severe strain, with high interception rates but continued penetrations, indicating a sustained aerial contest.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's air‑defence network has intensified interceptions, reporting near‑perfect shoot‑down ratios, yet civilian casualties continue to rise, prompting heightened alert levels in frontline oblasts. Russia has escalated attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure and deep‑strike operations into the Moscow region, signaling a willingness to expand the conflict's geographic scope. International observers note increased UAV activity beyond Ukraine's borders, as evidenced by Finnish detections of Ukrainian drones, suggesting broader regional air‑space implications.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed launch points in Herson and Zaporizhzhia is critical for pre‑emptive air‑defence positioning. The Iskander‑M threat from Crimea requires integration of missile‑defence assets in southern Ukraine. Continued UAV incursions into the Moscow region indicate Ukrainian capability for deep strikes, warranting diplomatic attention. High civilian casualty figures may affect domestic support and international aid flows, while the surge in Russian drone usage stresses supply chains for counter‑UAV systems.
